text.zh: 无人入池时，你的决策只剩两个：raise 或 fold。三原则：① 位置越靠后，开局范围越宽；② 统一加注尺寸（约 2.2–2.5bb），不随牌力变化；③ 不 open-limp——平跟既不能直接赢下底池，又放弃主动权、还给盲注免费看牌。
text.en: When the pot is unopened, your decision tree has two branches: raise or fold. Three rules: 1) the later your position, the wider you open; 2) use one raise size (about 2.2–2.5bb) regardless of hand strength; 3) never open-limp — limping cannot win the pot outright, surrenders initiative, and hands the blinds a free flop.

text.zh: CO 拿 A8s：UTG、HJ 相继弃牌轮到你——加注 2.3bb，标准开局。注意：同一手 A8s 在 UTG 是弃牌。变的不是牌，是后面还剩几个人。
text.en: You hold A8s in the cutoff: UTG and HJ fold to you — raise to 2.3bb, a standard open. Note that the very same A8s is a fold from UTG. The hand did not change; the number of players left to act did.

text.zh: 按「心情」调尺寸是新手最响的马脚：大牌下大注、投机牌下小注，等于把底牌写在脸上。另一个是偶尔 limp 大对子「设陷阱」——固定模式很快会被识破，还训练坏了自己的习惯。
text.en: Sizing by feel is the loudest beginner tell: big hands big, speculative hands small — you are writing your cards on your forehead. Another is the occasional trap-limp with big pairs: the pattern gets read fast and corrodes your own discipline.

text.zh: 训练目标是让每个位置的范围边界长成肌肉记忆。Drill 页的翻前训练器（M3 上线）就是为这件事造的。
text.en: The goal is to turn each position's range boundary into muscle memory. The preflop trainer on the Drill tab (arriving in M3) exists for exactly this.

text.zh: 各位置开局占比的训练近似：UTG ≈ 14%、HJ ≈ 17%、CO ≈ 26%、BTN ≈ 42%。范围向后位扩张有方向：先加入更多同花连张与带 A 的同花小牌，再加入更弱的 offsuit broadway。这些是训练基线，不是唯一正解。
text.en: Training approximations for opening frequency: UTG ≈ 14%, HJ ≈ 17%, CO ≈ 26%, BTN ≈ 42%. Ranges widen toward the back in a specific direction: first more suited connectors and small suited aces, then weaker offsuit broadways. These are training baselines, not the one true answer.

text.zh: K7s 是典型的「随位置解锁」的牌：UTG、HJ 弃，CO 边缘，BTN 标准开。一手牌横跨弃牌到标准开局，全看你坐在哪。
text.en: K7s is the textbook position-unlocked hand: fold from UTG and HJ, borderline in the CO, a standard open on the button. One hand spans fold to standard open depending purely on your seat.

text.zh: 把范围表当圣经逐格死背是低效的：先记「形状」与边界牌（每个位置最弱的开局牌），细节用 Matrix 工具反复对照校准。
text.en: Memorizing charts cell by cell is the slow road: learn the shape and the boundary hands (the weakest open at each seat) first, then calibrate details against the Matrix tool.

text.zh: 现在就去 Tools 看 UTG 与 BTN 两张范围表（M4 上线完整 Matrix），直观感受 14% 与 42% 的面积差——比任何文字都快。
text.en: Open the UTG and BTN charts under Tools (full Matrix arrives in M4) and let the area difference between 14% and 42% sink in — faster than any paragraph.

text.zh: 3-bet 是对开局加注的再加注，分两类：价值 3-bet——用范围顶端（QQ+、AK 一级）向更差的牌取价值；轻 3-bet——用 A5s 一类「阻断 + 可玩性」的牌施压偷池。尺寸基线：有位置约 3 倍开局额，无位置约 4 倍。
text.en: A 3-bet re-raises the open. Two families: value 3-bets — the top of your range (QQ+, AK tier) charging worse hands; light 3-bets — hands like A5s with blockers plus playability applying pressure. Sizing baseline: about 3x the open in position, 4x out of position.

text.zh: BTN 开 2.5bb，你在 BB 拿 A5s：3-bet 到约 10bb。那张 A 阻断对手的 AA/AK，被跟注后还有同花与顺子潜力兜底。同样的尺寸拿 AQs/AK 也加——目的换成了价值。
text.en: BTN opens 2.5bb, you hold A5s in the BB: 3-bet to about 10bb. The ace blocks AA/AK, and if called you fall back on flush and wheel potential. Same size with AQs/AK — the purpose flips to value.

text.zh: 只用 QQ+ 3-bet 会让范围透明——对手对你的再加注一律弃牌止损，你的大牌赚不到钱。反向错误是用 KJo 这类中段牌 3-bet：价值不够、阻断一般、被跟注后处处尴尬。
text.en: 3-betting only QQ+ makes you transparent — opponents simply fold to your re-raises and your monsters earn nothing. The mirror error is 3-betting middling hands like KJo: not enough value, mediocre blockers, awkward everywhere when called.

text.zh: 入门口径：价值用范围顶端，轻 3-bet 优先选带 A 的同花小牌；中段牌走跟注或弃牌。先把这条线打直，再谈混合。
text.en: Starter policy: value with the top of range, light 3-bets preferring small suited aces; middling hands call or fold. Straighten this line first — mixing comes later.

title.zh: 面对开局加注：三选项框架
title.en: Facing a Raise: The Three-Option Frame

text.zh: 前面有人开局加注时，你只有三个选项：弃牌、跟注、3-bet。跟注需要好的可玩性与位置支撑；3-bet 范围以强牌为主体，掺入少量带阻断与同花潜力的牌；其余一律弃牌——纪律本身就是利润来源。
text.en: When someone opens in front of you, there are only three options: fold, call, or 3-bet. Calling needs playability and position; the 3-bet range is built on strong hands plus a few blockers with suited potential; everything else folds — discipline itself is where the edge comes from.

text.zh: CO 开局 2.5bb，你在 BTN 拿 KQs：跟注与 3-bet 在训练表里都站得住。同样位置换成 A9o，则进入「看似漂亮、实则被支配」的弃牌区——它撞上 CO 的 AT+、AJ+ 时几乎全程落后。
text.en: CO opens to 2.5bb and you hold KQs on the BTN: both calling and 3-betting are defensible in the training charts. Swap in A9o and you are in the 'pretty but dominated' fold zone — against CO's AT+ and AJ+ it is behind almost everywhere.

text.zh: 常见错误：用 A9o、KJo 这类被支配的牌冷跟开局加注。它们经常中对子还输——翻后亏损来自翻前的一念之差。
text.en: Common mistake: cold-calling opens with dominated hands like A9o or KJo. They often make a pair and still lose — postflop bleed that started with one preflop click.

text.zh: 位置越靠后越能跟注；没有位置时，倾向「3-bet 或弃牌」，让局面保持简单。
text.en: The later your position, the more you can call; without position, lean 3-bet-or-fold and keep the tree simple.

title.zh: 盲注攻防：SB 与 BB 为什么不同
title.en: Blind Battles: Why SB and BB Differ

text.zh: SB 是全桌最差位置：翻后永远先行动，所以训练表把它简化为「3-bet 或弃牌」。BB 则相反——已经投入一个盲注、收盘行动、跟注有折扣，因此防守范围全桌最宽。
text.en: The SB is the worst seat at the table: it acts first on every postflop street, so the training charts simplify it to 3-bet-or-fold. The BB is the opposite — money already in, closing the action, calling at a discount — so it defends the widest range of any seat.

text.zh: BTN 开局，你在 SB 拿 K9o：弃牌（不在 3-bet 表里，平跟会把自己挤在两个位置劣势之间）。同一手牌坐在 BB：跟注防守完全成立。
text.en: BTN opens and you hold K9o in the SB: fold (it is not in the 3-bet chart, and flatting squeezes you between two positional disadvantages). The same hand in the BB is a perfectly fine defend.

text.zh: 常见错误：SB 习惯性平跟「不想浪费半个盲注」。半个盲注是沉没成本；为它再搭上整个翻后劣势，才是真正的浪费。
text.en: Common mistake: habitually flatting from the SB 'to not waste the half blind already posted.' That half blind is sunk; paying a full postflop disadvantage to chase it is the real waste.

text.zh: 记口诀：SB 要么打回去，要么放手；BB 才是跟注防守的主场。
text.en: Mantra: from the SB, fight back or let go; the BB is where call-defense lives.

title.en: The Three Core Stats: VPIP · PFR · AF

text.zh: VPIP 是主动投钱入池的比例，PFR 是翻前加注比例，AF 是（下注+加注）÷ 跟注的进攻系数。三者合起来回答两个问题：他玩多少牌？他用这些牌做什么？
text.en: VPIP is how often a player voluntarily puts money in, PFR how often they raise preflop, and AF the ratio of (bets + raises) to calls. Together they answer two questions: how many hands does this player play, and what do they do with them?

text.zh: 一位 22/18、AF 2.5 的对手是教科书 TAG：玩得少、进攻多。另一位 45/8 的对手——什么都跟、几乎不加注——是典型的 Calling Station 信号。
text.en: A 22/18 player with AF 2.5 is a textbook TAG: few hands, lots of aggression. A 45/8 player — calling everything, raising almost nothing — is the classic calling-station signature.

text.zh: 常见错误：把 VPIP 和 PFR 孤立来看。45 的 VPIP 单独看只是「松」，配上 8 的 PFR 才读出「松而被动」这个真正可剥削的画像。
text.en: Common mistake: reading VPIP or PFR in isolation. A 45 VPIP alone just says 'loose'; only paired with an 8 PFR does it spell 'loose-passive' — the actually exploitable profile.

text.zh: 先看 VPIP−PFR 的差值：差距越大，对手越爱跟注，你的价值下注就越能做厚。
text.en: Check the VPIP−PFR gap first: the wider it is, the more this player calls, and the thicker your value bets can be.

text.zh: 指标是统计量，统计量需要样本。30 手以下，VPIP 这类数字的噪声大到几乎不可用；这正是本应用的分类器在样本不足时拒绝下结论的原因。
text.en: Stats are statistics, and statistics need samples. Under 30 hands, numbers like VPIP are mostly noise — which is exactly why this app's classifier refuses to label anyone on a thin sample.

text.zh: 对手前 10 手玩了 6 手（VPIP 60%）？可能只是连续拿到好牌。再看 50 手，数字往往回落到 25 上下——人没变，样本变了。
text.en: An opponent plays 6 of their first 10 hands (60% VPIP)? They may simply have been dealt a heater. Watch 50 hands and the number often settles near 25 — the player did not change, the sample did.

text.zh: 常见错误：三手牌就给人定型，然后按错误画像做大决定。早期标签贴得越快，撕下来越疼。
text.en: Common mistake: typecasting someone after three hands, then making big decisions off the wrong read. The faster the early label goes on, the more it hurts to peel off.

text.zh: 样本不足时，回到基础策略，把对手当未知打——这正是范围表存在的意义。
text.en: On a thin sample, fall back to baseline strategy and play them as unknown — that is exactly what the charts are for.

prompt.zh: 无人入池轮到你，标准的决策集合是？
prompt.en: Folded to you in an unopened pot — the standard decision set is?

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：开局局面是 raise-or-fold——要么施压，要么放弃。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: unopened pots are raise-or-fold — apply pressure or let it go.

choiceExplanations.zh: open-limp 不能直接赢锅、放弃主动权、给盲注免费牌，本训练体系不采用。
choiceExplanations.en: Open-limping cannot win the pot outright, surrenders initiative and gifts the blinds a free flop — this curriculum does not use it.

choiceExplanations.zh: 同一手牌在不同位置可以从弃牌变成标准开局——位置是决策的一半。
choiceExplanations.en: The same hand swings from fold to standard open by seat — position is half the decision.

objective.zh: 确立 raise-or-fold 的开局纪律。

prompt.zh: 开局加注的基线尺寸约为？
prompt.en: The baseline open-raise size is about?

choiceExplanations.zh: 1bb 等于 limp 的价格，达不到施压目的。
choiceExplanations.en: 1bb is limp money — it applies no pressure.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：约 2.2–2.5bb，并且全范围统一，不随牌力起伏。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: about 2.2–2.5bb, one size for the whole range, never keyed to hand strength.

choiceExplanations.zh: 5bb 把自己的边缘开局牌全做成了大锅深坑。
choiceExplanations.en: 5bb turns every marginal open into an oversized trap for yourself.

prompt.zh: 从 UTG 到 BTN，开局范围如何变化？
prompt.en: From UTG to the button, how does the opening range change?

choiceExplanations.zh: 方向反了：身后的对手越少，能开的牌越多。
choiceExplanations.en: Backwards: fewer players behind means more hands to open.

choiceExplanations.zh: UTG≈14% 到 BTN≈42%，是三倍量级的差距。
choiceExplanations.en: UTG ≈ 14% versus BTN ≈ 42% — a threefold gap.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：从约 14% 放宽到约 42%（训练近似）。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: from about 14% to about 42% (training approximation).

objective.zh: 建立范围随位置扩张的直觉。

prompt.zh: BTN 的开局范围大约覆盖全部起手牌的？
prompt.en: The button's opening range covers roughly what share of all hands?

choiceExplanations.zh: 15% 是 UTG 量级的占比。
choiceExplanations.en: 15% is UTG territory.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：约 42%（训练近似）——最好的位置开最宽的范围。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: about 42% (training approximation) — the best seat opens the widest range.

choiceExplanations.zh: 70% 连 72o 的邻居都要进场了——宽范围仍然弃掉最差的一半。
choiceExplanations.en: 70% would invite 72o's neighbors — even wide ranges fold the worse half.

prompt.zh: 轻 3-bet（bluff 性质）优先选哪类牌？
prompt.en: Which hands make the best light (bluff-leaning) 3-bets?

choices.zh: A5s 这类阻断 + 同花潜力的牌
choices.en: Hands like A5s — blockers plus suited potential
Any two cards — it is about aggression

choiceExplanations.zh: 中段牌 3-bet 两头不靠：价值不足、被跟后难打——它们更适合跟注或弃牌。
choiceExplanations.en: Middling hands sit in no-man's land: not enough value, awkward when called — they prefer call or fold.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：A 阻断对手最强的跟注/反加范围，同花小 A 被跟后还有可玩性兜底。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: the ace blocks the strongest continue range, and the suited wheel ace keeps playability when called.

choiceExplanations.zh: 无选择的激进只是更快的输法。
choiceExplanations.en: Unselective aggression is just a faster way to lose.

objective.zh: 理解阻断牌在轻 3-bet 中的作用。

prompt.zh: 无位置（如 BB 对 BTN）3-bet 的尺寸基线约为开局额的？
prompt.en: Out of position (e.g. BB vs BTN), the 3-bet sizing baseline is about?

choiceExplanations.zh: 2 倍给了对手近乎完美的跟注赔率，位置劣势雪上加霜。
choiceExplanations.en: 2x hands the opener near-perfect odds — painful when you are also out of position.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：无位置约 4 倍（有位置约 3 倍），用尺寸补偿位置劣势。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: about 4x out of position (3x in position) — size compensates for the seat.

choiceExplanations.zh: 6 倍把自己的轻 3-bet 全变成高价诈唬，代价失衡。
choiceExplanations.en: 6x makes every light 3-bet an overpriced bluff — the cost no longer balances.

objective.zh: 记住 IP 3 倍 / OOP 4 倍的尺寸基线。
objective.en: Anchor 3x in position, 4x out of position.

prompt.zh: CO 开局加注，你在 BTN 拿 A9o。三选项框架下最有纪律的选择是？
prompt.en: CO opens and you hold A9o on the BTN. Under the three-option frame, the disciplined play is?

choiceExplanations.zh: A9o 撞上 CO 的 AT+ 几乎全程被支配，跟注是慢性失血。
choiceExplanations.en: A9o 'is dominated by CO's AT+ almost everywhere; calling bleeds slowly.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：看似漂亮、实则被支配的牌，弃牌就是利润。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: pretty-but-dominated hands make money by folding.

choiceExplanations.zh: 它既没有足够牌力做价值，也缺少同花潜力做诈唬，3-bet 两头不靠。
choiceExplanations.en: It lacks the strength for value and the suited potential for a bluff — a 3-bet serves neither goal.

objective.zh: 识别被支配牌，守住三选项纪律。
objective.en: Spot dominated hands and keep three-option discipline.

prompt.zh: 3-Bet 范围里掺入 A5s 这类牌，主要理由是什么？
prompt.en: Why do hands like A5s belong in a 3-bet range?

choices.zh: 阻断 AA / AK，且有同花与顺子潜力
choices.en: It blocks AA/AK and has suited, straight potential
It performs best as a call

choiceExplanations.zh: A5 中顶对的频率很低，这不是选它的原因。
choiceExplanations.en: A5 rarely flops top pair; that is not the point.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：持有 A 让对手更难拿到 AA/AK，被跟注时还有同花、轮子顺的后路。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: holding an ace makes opponent AA/AK rarer, and when called you keep flush and wheel equity.

choiceExplanations.zh: 作为跟注它常被支配；它的价值恰恰在于当 3-bet 的进攻翼。
choiceExplanations.en: As a call it is often dominated; its value is precisely as the aggressive wing of a 3-bet range.

objective.zh: 理解 3-bet 诈唬翼的选牌逻辑。
objective.en: Understand how the bluff wing of a 3-bet range is chosen.

prompt.zh: 为什么 BB 是全桌防守最宽的位置？
prompt.en: Why does the BB defend the widest range at the table?

choices.zh: 已投入盲注、收盘行动、跟注有折扣
choices.en: Money already posted, closes the action, calls at a discount
The BB is dealt better cards

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：三个结构性因素叠加，让许多边缘牌的防守价格变得划算。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: three structural factors stack up to make marginal defends priced in.

choiceExplanations.zh: 恰恰相反，BB 翻后几乎总是没有位置。
choiceExplanations.en: Quite the opposite — the BB is almost always out of position postflop.

choiceExplanations.zh: 发牌对每个位置都是均匀的，差异来自结构而非运气。
choiceExplanations.en: Cards are dealt evenly to every seat; the difference is structural, not luck.

objective.zh: 说出 BB 宽防守的三个结构性原因。
objective.en: Name the structural reasons behind wide BB defense.

prompt.zh: BTN 开局，你在 SB 拿 JTs。按训练表的口径，动作是？
prompt.en: BTN opens and you hold JTs in the SB. Per the training charts, the action is?

choiceExplanations.zh: SB 训练表里没有平跟这个选项——平跟会把你挤在双重位置劣势里。
choiceExplanations.en: The SB chart has no flatting option — a flat wedges you into a double positional disadvantage.

choiceExplanations.zh: JTs 的同花顺潜力足以进入进攻翼，直接放弃太可惜。
choiceExplanations.en: JTs has enough suited-connector potential for the aggressive wing; folding gives up too much.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：SB 采用「3-bet 或弃牌」，JTs 在 3-bet 表内。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: the SB plays 3-bet-or-fold, and JTs is in the 3-bet chart.

objective.zh: 内化 SB 的 3-bet 或弃牌纪律。

prompt.zh: 对手数据 VPIP 45 / PFR 8。这组数字最像哪类玩家？
prompt.en: An opponent runs VPIP 45 / PFR 8. This profile most resembles?

choiceExplanations.zh: TAG 的画像是低 VPIP、高 PFR，与这组数字相反。
choiceExplanations.en: A TAG runs low VPIP with high PFR — the opposite of these numbers.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：玩一半的牌却几乎不加注——什么都想看、什么都跟。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: playing half the hands while almost never raising — wants to see everything, calls everything.

choiceExplanations.zh: Maniac 同样很松，但 PFR 也高、不停施压，与 8 的 PFR 不符。
choiceExplanations.en: A maniac is also loose but with high PFR and relentless pressure — 8 PFR does not fit.

objective.zh: 把 VPIP/PFR 组合映射到玩家类型。
objective.en: Map VPIP/PFR pairs to player types.

prompt.zh: AF（进攻系数）衡量的是什么？

choices.en: How often a player enters pots
Bets plus raises relative to calls

choiceExplanations.zh: 入池频率是 VPIP 的领地。
choiceExplanations.en: Entering pots is VPIP's territory.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：AF 回答「他投钱时是主动进攻还是被动跟随」。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: AF answers whether money goes in aggressively or passively.

choiceExplanations.zh: 翻前加注频率由 PFR 描述。
choiceExplanations.en: Preflop raising is described by PFR.

objective.zh: 区分三大指标各自的职责。
objective.en: Keep the three core stats in their own lanes.

prompt.zh: 只观察了对手 12 手牌，他的 VPIP 显示 58%。最合理的用法是？
prompt.en: You have watched only 12 hands and the opponent's VPIP shows 58%. The right way to use this is?

choices.en: Exploit them as ultra-loose right away

choices.zh: 仅作参考，先按基础策略应对
choices.en: Treat it as a hint and stay on baseline strategy

choiceExplanations.zh: 12 手的噪声可以让任何人看起来像疯子或岩石。
choiceExplanations.en: Twelve hands of noise can make anyone look like a maniac or a rock.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：小样本只配做假设，不配做结论；先按未知对手打。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: thin samples earn hypotheses, not conclusions; play them as unknown for now.

choiceExplanations.zh: 数据并非无用——样本到位后它是最可靠的朋友。
choiceExplanations.en: Stats are not useless — once the sample arrives they are your most reliable friend.

objective.zh: 学会按样本大小给读数定权重。

prompt.zh: 按本应用的口径，指标大约从多少手起才开始有参考价值？
prompt.en: Per this app's convention, stats start to mean something after roughly how many hands?

choiceExplanations.zh: 5 手连「他在不在玩牌」都说不清。
choiceExplanations.en: Five hands cannot even tell whether they are playing or folding.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：30 手是分类器开始给出判断的最低样本线。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: 30 hands is the classifier's minimum line before it offers a read.

choiceExplanations.zh: 1000 手当然更准，但实战中等不起；30 手已能给出方向。
choiceExplanations.en: A thousand hands is better, but live play cannot wait; 30 already points a direction.

objective.zh: 记住 sampleMin = 30 的工程与统计含义。
